events.description: Senator James E. Murray introduced S. 716, a bill authorizing issuance of a patent in fee to George Peters, read twice and referred to the Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs
George Peters wrote a letter to Senator Murray (referenced in Murray's January 26 reply)
Senator Murray replied to George Peters advising he had prepared and was introducing a new bill to authorize issuance of a patent in fee
Department of the Interior (Oscar Chapman, Undersecretary) issued report on S. 716 recommending enactment if amended to allow sale to a Crow Indian under existing regulations

Senator Joseph C. O'Mahoney, Chairman of Senate Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs, wrote to Senator Murray attaching Department of Interior report on S. 716 and asking if amendments were agreeable
S. 716 passed the United States Senate
House Committee on Public Lands reported S. 716 favorably without amendment, committed to the Committee of the Whole House and ordered to be printed (Report No. 508)
Senator Murray wrote to George Peters advising that S. 716 passed the Senate on April 11th and had been reported favorably out of the House Committee on Public Lands, enclosing copy of House report
S. 716 passed the House of Representatives and was cleared for President's signature
Senator James E. Murray sent a Day Letter telegram via Western Union to George Peters informing him that S. 716 passed the House and was cleared for President's signature
George Peters indicated his desire to sell his land to George Redfield, another Crow Indian
Senate Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs amended S. 716 at suggestion of Department of the Interior to permit Secretary to sell land under existing regulations rather than issue patent in fee

financial_transactions.description: Proposed sale of 840 acres (Allotment No. 2292) on Crow Indian Reservation from George Peters to a Crow Indian under existing regulations, authorized by amended S. 716
